title: Int values ending in 'e' not handled the same as MySQL
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

```sql
create table t (id int primary key, nn bigint);
insert into t values (1, '5e');
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
mysql-9.3.0> create table t (id int primary key, nn bigint);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.023 sec)

mysql-9.3.0> insert into t values (1, '5e');
Query OK, 1 row affected (0.012 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
mysql-8.0.11-TiDB-v9.0.0-alpha-578-g81b5f5170a> create table t (id int primary key, nn bigint);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.009 sec)

mysql-8.0.11-TiDB-v9.0.0-alpha-578-g81b5f5170a> insert into t values (1, '5e');
ERROR 1366 (HY000): Incorrect bigint value: '5e' for column 'nn' at row 1
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

```
Release Version: v9.0.0-alpha-591-g9806098a9e-dirty
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 9806098a9ee2a18399882c27f7e519b8ed159f3a
Git Branch: master
UTC Build Time: 2025-04-18 22:17:54
GoVersion: go1.24.2
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: unistore
```
poc: create table t (id int primary key, nn bigint);
key_bug_features: Inserting '5e' as bigint fails in TiDB
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value BIGINT NOT NULL, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, description) VALUES (1, '5e', 'Test with scientific notation');
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id) VALUES (1, (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value = '5e'));
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
